notes: Outline exercises TWO policy paths — `propose` mode (3 candidate outlines, selection block) and `direct` mode (save a chosen outline). DPs whose answer cleanly splits by mode use a structured `proposal` object: `{"propose": "...", "direct": "..."}`. DPs whose content mixes mode-specific + shared (DP-4, DP-10) stay as plain strings so the cross-mode commentary stays in one place. The app renders `typeof proposal === 'object'` as two stacked textareas labelled per mode; strings render as one textarea.

schema.applies_when: the flow has multiple policy modes whose answers diverge cleanly

DP-1.title: Flow-specific must/never rules
DP-1.question: 0–3 inviolable rules unique to this flow, pinned in the skill template.
DP-1.proposal: 1) In propose mode, MUST NOT call `generate_outline` (user hasn't chosen yet); emit exactly 3 options as markdown text. 2) In direct mode, MUST call `generate_outline` exactly once after composing the outline. 3) Vary the angles across propose-mode options (e.g., listicle vs. narrative vs. how-to) — three near-identical outlines defeat the purpose of propose.
DP-1.reasoning: The propose/direct tool split is the bug hot spot: policy has a detection fallback (lines 86–90) but the skill must enforce. Varying angles is the quality gate on propose.

DP-2.title: Skill intro paragraph
DP-2.question: The one- or two-sentence framing that opens the skill file.
DP-2.proposal: This skill describes how to generate a fresh outline for a post. You operate in one of two modes, signaled by the `stage` field in `<resolved_details>`: `propose` means produce 3 candidate outlines as text for the user to pick from; `direct` means save the chosen outline via `generate_outline`.
DP-2.reasoning: Opening must make the bimodal nature unmissable since the mode gates the tool call. The `stage` field is the unambiguous signal.

DP-3.title: Process steps
DP-3.question: Numbered happy-path sequence for `## Process`.

proposal.propose:
1. Read `stage='propose'` from `<resolved_details>`.
2. Optionally call `find_posts(query=<topic>)` ONCE to scan for existing posts on the topic (vary angles against what's already been written).
3. Emit exactly 3 outline options as markdown. Use the strict format `### Option N` + `## <section title>` + 1–2 sentence description per section. 4–7 sections per option. No trailing commentary.
4. DO NOT call `generate_outline`. End the turn with text.
proposal.direct:
1. Read `stage='direct'` from `<resolved_details>`.
2. Read the section list from `<resolved_details>` (either user-named sections or the chosen option's sections).
3. Draft bullets per section honoring the `depth` slot: depth=1 → flat bullets; depth=2 → `### Sub-section` + bullets; depth=3 → `### Sub-section` + `- bullet` + `  * sub-bullet` (per UA-4).
4. Call `generate_outline(post_id, content=<markdown outline>)` to save. End the turn.

DP-3.reasoning: Branch-on-stage is the clearest workflow for a bimodal flow. Each mode has independent steps; the direct branch wires the `depth` slot which inventory flagged as unused (gap #2).

DP-4.title: Tool usage hints
DP-4.question: Per task-specific tool, 1–2 flow-specific usage hints.
DP-4.proposal:
- `find_posts(query)` — optional in BOTH modes, but AT MOST ONCE. Use to vary angles (propose) or to ground against prior work (direct). If it returns nothing, proceed without it.
- `generate_outline(post_id, content)` — direct mode only, exactly once at the end of the turn. Pass the full markdown outline; the tool replaces any existing outline.
- `read_metadata(post_id)` — do not call; post context is pre-resolved in `<resolved_details>`.
DP-4.reasoning: String-shaped because the at-most-once cap on `find_posts` spans both modes; splitting would duplicate that cross-mode invariant. Explicit `read_metadata` ban prevents the redundant call pattern.

DP-5.title: Task framing sentence
DP-5.question: The imperative sentence inside `<task>`.

proposal.propose: Propose 3 outline options for "{title}" on the topic "{topic}". Vary the angle across options. Emit each as markdown per the format; do not save. End once all 3 options have been emitted.
proposal.direct: Generate an outline for "{title}" using the section list below. Compose bullets per section honoring the requested depth, then call `generate_outline` to save. End once the outline has been saved.

DP-5.reasoning: Two task strings, one per mode, selected by the starter module based on `flow.stage`. Both include the termination clause per UA convention.

DP-6.title: Content tag choice
DP-6.question: Which of `<post_content>`, `<section_content>`, `<line_snippet>`, `<channel_content>`.
DP-6.reasoning: Outline targets the post entity in both modes. In propose mode the tag wraps the topic + any existing outline (empty is fine). In direct mode it wraps the chosen section list.

DP-7.title: Preload content
DP-7.question: What goes inside the content tag.

proposal.propose: Topic, post title, and an empty or placeholder outline structure. Format: `<topic: User Simulators for RL training>\n<existing_sections: none>`.
proposal.direct:
Section list as markdown headings without bullets (the skill fills them), plus the `depth` slot value. Format: `<depth: 1>
## Motivation
## Architecture
## Evaluation`.

DP-7.reasoning: Propose needs topic context for angle variation; direct needs section names + depth hint. Keeping payloads small (under 500 tokens) avoids burning budget on an empty post.

DP-8.title: Scenario coverage
DP-8.question: Which user-phrasing paths to exemplify (typically 3 per skill).
DP-8.proposal: (a) propose-mode on a fresh post with a topic and no existing outline — 3 options with distinct angles; (b) direct-mode with user-named sections (Motivation / Architecture / Evaluation) and depth=1; (c) direct-mode via propose-then-pick: `proposals` slot filled with user's choice, depth=2 with sub-sections.
DP-8.reasoning: Covers both eval substeps (02a propose, 02b direct-via-pick) plus the standalone direct path (step 2). The depth=2 case exercises the UA-4 scheme that DP-3 encoded. Scenarios are a portfolio, not a per-mode choice — one list is appropriate.

DP-9.title: Example content (topic + post state)
DP-9.question: Concrete post topic, section names, and content.
DP-9.proposal: Topic: "User Simulators for training RL agents" (post_id=abcd0123). Example sections: Motivation / Architecture / Evaluation. Eval uses the same topic but different sections (Motivation / Process / Ideas / Takeaways) — keep divergence to avoid contamination. Propose-mode example shows 3 distinct angle presentations: (1) academic survey, (2) engineering walkthrough, (3) practitioner checklist.
DP-9.reasoning: Consistent with the Batch-1 corpus. Distinct angles are the whole point of propose mode — the example must demonstrate variance, not just three outlines with the same headings.

DP-10.title: Likely errors and ambiguities encountered
DP-10.question: Realistic (violation × recovery) and (ambiguity level × trigger) set.
DP-10.proposal: Errors: (a) LLM called `generate_outline` in propose mode → policy catches (line 86) and reclassifies as direct success, no user-visible error. (b) LLM emitted fewer than 3 options or malformed markdown in propose → `parse_failure` error frame (candidate parsing fails in policy line 92). (c) `generate_outline` fails in direct mode after 1 retry → `tool_error` frame. Ambiguities: (d) `source` slot not filled → `partial` (shared across modes). (e) `topic` not extractable from conversation AND no `sections` → `specific`, `missing_slot='topic'` (propose path only; direct needs sections already).
DP-10.reasoning: String-shaped because ambiguity (d) is shared across modes and (e) straddles mode-entry logic — splitting would obscure which errors are mode-specific vs. flow-wide. Mirrors current behavior in outline_policy plus the AD-6 error-frame contract.

DP-11.title: Fallback flow
DP-11.question: Does another flow take over on unworkable state?
DP-11.proposal: No fallback. Outline may NOT stack on itself (AD-3). On unrecoverable state, emit an error frame and let the user retry with clearer input.
DP-11.reasoning: Outline is already the base outline-producing flow; nothing more fundamental to stack on.

DP-12.title: Retry strategy per tool
DP-12.question: Per tool, retry count on transient failure.
DP-12.proposal: `generate_outline` → retry once via `BasePolicy.retry_tool`, then `execution_error('tool_error', failed_tool='generate_outline')`. `find_posts` → no retry (optional tool; proceed without results on failure).
DP-12.reasoning: Standard 1-retry on the save path. `find_posts` is best-effort; a flake shouldn't stall the turn.

DP-13.title: Optional slot defaults (AD-8)
DP-13.question: For each optional slot, does it have a sensible default?
DP-13.proposal: `depth` (LevelSlot, optional, threshold=1): default to 1 (flat bullets). Per AD-8, commit with default and don't ask. `topic`, `sections`, `proposals`: elective (not optional) — the elective rule requires at least one and determines propose vs. direct mode, so no default applies.
DP-13.reasoning: depth=1 is the canonical starting outline shape; asking about depth is friction. Elective slots are definitional (flow mode switch), not defaultable.

DP-14.title: Stack-on triggers
DP-14.question: Under what condition does this flow push another flow onto the stack?
DP-14.proposal: None. Outline is the producer of outlines; nothing prerequisites it. Per AD-3, outline MUST NOT stack on itself.
DP-14.reasoning: Self-recursion is gated by the `sections`-filled branch, not `stackon`. This is a documented invariant (AD-3).

DP-15.title: Scratchpad contract
DP-15.question: Does this flow write or read scratchpad?
DP-15.proposal: No scratchpad interaction. Propose mode writes candidates to `flow.slots['proposals'].options` (in-flow state, not scratchpad); the recursion picks them up on the same turn or the next selection turn.
DP-15.reasoning: Propose-to-direct handoff is a same-flow thing; the scratchpad is for cross-flow findings (per AD-1). ProposalSlot is the right channel.

DP-16.title: Frame output spec (metadata keys + block types)
DP-16.question: What metadata keys does the `DisplayFrame` carry, and what block types?

proposal.propose: Success: `origin='outline'`; `metadata={stage: 'propose', option_count: 3}`; blocks = 1 `selection` block listing the 3 options. Error: `origin='outline'`; `metadata={violation: 'parse_failure'}`; blocks empty; `code` = the raw LLM markdown that failed to parse.
proposal.direct: Success: `origin='outline'`; `metadata={stage: 'direct', post_id, post_title, section_ids}`; blocks = 1 `card` block with full post content. Error: `origin='outline'`; `metadata={violation: 'tool_error', failed_tool: 'generate_outline'}`; blocks empty.

DP-16.reasoning: Structured per mode because success block types differ (`selection` vs. `card`) and error violations differ (`parse_failure` on propose parsing vs. `tool_error` on direct save). `stage` in metadata tells RES which template path to render.

DP-17.title: Final reply shape
DP-17.question: Plain prose, structured JSON, or card-only?

proposal.propose: Plain prose, 1 sentence pointing the user at the options ("Here are three ways to approach this — pick one."). The selection block does the work.
proposal.direct: Plain prose, 1–2 sentences noting what was saved ("Saved a 4-section outline for <title>."). Card block carries the structured data.

DP-17.reasoning: Persona compliance (1–2 sentence max) applies to both; the text content is mode-specific because the blocks differ.

DP-18.title: Model tier override
DP-18.question: Opus over Sonnet default?
DP-18.proposal: Sonnet (default) for both modes. Propose-mode quality is about angle diversity, not prose polish — Sonnet handles angle selection well enough. Direct mode is a structured save, also Sonnet-appropriate.
DP-18.reasoning: Neither mode is prose-quality-gated. Opus reserved for polish/rework per DP-18 precedent. Single answer because both modes land on the same tier.

DP-19.title: max_response_tokens override
DP-19.question: Tighten below 4096 default?

proposal.propose: 3072 — 3 options × ~7 sections × 2 sentences ≈ 2000 tokens of markdown; headroom for longer angle descriptions.
proposal.direct: 2048 — matches refine; outline size is bounded by ~20 sections.

DP-19.reasoning: Propose is the only flow in Batch 1 that generates 3x the content of direct flows. Different caps per mode avoid over-paying for the cheaper path.
